metadata.title: Claude replaced me.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: this is Liam, in for Bear. A reusable Claude Skill can package the guidance that once required a human teacher. The deeper question is what remains uniquely human when procedural instruction becomes a slash command.

props.command: Can a Claude Skill package a teacher's repeatable guidance—and what remains human?

beats.narration_text: Here is the artifact: a Claude Skill called /how-to. You install it from a shared skill file, upload it into Claude, and from then on you just type /how-to plus whatever you want to do with AI — and Claude walks you through it, step by step, asking you the right questions before giving you a plan. The claim is that procedural guidance can live inside the Skill instead of being rediscovered across dozens of articles.

artifactLines: Download from Dropbox → upload into Claude → invoke with /how-to.
Claude asks clarifying questions, then lays out a full plan.
Works for any Claude task, even ones you've never tried before.
the claim: reusable guidance can move from a newsletter into an invokable Skill.

beats.narration_text: Step back for a second: what is a Claude skill, technically? It's a pre-made prompt — a file of instructions — that teaches Claude how to behave for a specific task. You install it once, then call it by name with a slash command. No more repeating your context. No more pasting the same prompt. the /how-to skill is just that: a file that tells Claude to guide a user from zero to done on any Claude task.

artifactLines: A skill = a file of instructions Claude loads before answering.
You call it with a slash: /how-to, /linkedin-post, /email-draft.
Install once. Use forever. Share with your whole team.
the /how-to Skill: Claude becomes an on-demand procedural tutor.

beats.narration_text: The install takes four steps. Download the shared Skill file. Then inside Claude: Customize → Skills → plus sign → Create skill → Upload a skill. Select the file. Done. Open a new chat, type /how-to, add what you want to accomplish, and Claude starts asking you questions instead of waiting for a perfect prompt from you.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B03.png (screenshot of Claude Skills install flow: Customize → Skills → + → Upload a skill, or the Dropbox download screen)

beats.narration_text: There's a technique inside the skill that This can be understood as out by name: AskUserQuestion. Instead of you trying to write the perfect prompt, Claude becomes the interviewer. It asks three to five targeted questions. The more you answer, the better the plan it gives back. In practice, this one move — making Claude ask YOU the questions first — puts you ahead of ninety-nine-point-nine percent of AI users. And he's not wrong.

artifactLines: Claude asks YOU the questions. Not the other way around.
3-5 targeted questions before any plan gets written.
The more you answer, the sharper the output.
Shortcut: add ELI5 if you're stuck on a step — Claude dials back the complexity.

beats.narration_text: Power users can add a stronger reasoning layer. Don't use the regular chat view. Open Claude Cowork instead. Select Opus model at High effort. Then invoke /how-to. The reasoning engine runs longer, the questions go deeper, and the plan that comes back is more thorough. Solo users get a fine result in Chat mode — but Cowork plus Opus is the ceiling. That's the strongest stack for running the skill.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B05.png (screenshot of Claude Cowork tab with model selector showing Opus at High effort, /how-to invoked)

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Download the skill, install it, and try it on the Claude task you've been putting off. Paste this prompt to kick it off.

props.command: /how-to I want to [describe the thing you've been putting off with Claude]. Ask me what you need first.

beats.narration_text: The Skill is practical. Install it, inspect it, and adapt it.
